title: Educational Events Gallery with Submissions and Transparent Review Overlay (Frontend)

requirements.summary: Build an events gallery view that loads events from the backend and renders photo cards with a transparent-styled review overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: The page loads events from the backend and renders them without requiring a page refresh.

Each event card displays the photo prominently and the review text is visually transparent (photo visible through the glyphs) while remaining readable (e.g., via subtle stroke/shadow/blurred backdrop or similar).
If no events exist, the UI shows an English empty-state message (e.g., “No events yet. Be the first to submit one.”).

file_operations.description: Create the gallery page that uses React Query to load events via the backend actor (listEvents) and renders a responsive grid/list. Show an English empty-state when the list is empty.

Create an event photo card component that renders the image and overlays the review text with transparent styling (photo visible behind text) while preserving readability (e.g., subtle stroke/shadow/backdrop).
Add a React Query hook that reads events from the backend actor using useActor and listEvents, returning loading/error states suitable for the gallery.
Create a helper component for rendering event images using the blob-storage ExternalBlob (use getDirectURL for display where possible). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add top-level app composition that wires navigation between the Gallery view and the Submit Event view (no authentication required). Ensure the Gallery view is accessible and is the default view.

requirements.summary: Implement a Submit Event form with validation and image upload that submits to the backend and refreshes the gallery on success.
acceptanceCriteria: The form validates required fields (at minimum: title, review text, and image) and shows English validation messages.
The image upload accepts common formats (JPEG/PNG/WebP at minimum) and is converted to the backend’s expected payload (bytes + MIME type).
After successful submission, the user sees an English success confirmation and the new event is visible in the gallery list without manual reload (e.g., via React Query invalidation/refetch).
On backend error, the user sees an English error message and the event is not added to the gallery.

file_operations.description: Create the Submit Event page containing the form fields (title, description, optional date, optional location, review, image upload), English validation messages, and success/error confirmation UI.

Implement the form UI using existing UI primitives (shadcn-ui components) by composing them (do not modify frontend/src/components/ui/*). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add a React Query mutation hook that calls the backend actor createEvent, shows submission state, and invalidates/refetches the events query so the new event appears without a manual reload.

Add utilities to validate file type (JPEG/PNG/WebP), read the selected image as bytes, and construct a blob-storage ExternalBlob from bytes for backend submission. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Wire the Submit Event page into navigation alongside the Gallery. Ensure post-submit success routes back (or switches) to the Gallery view and triggers an events refresh.

requirements.summary: Apply a cohesive educational/professional theme across gallery and submission views using Tailwind and existing UI primitives, avoiding blue/purple as primary colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Gallery and submission pages share consistent typography, spacing, and component styling.
Primary palette is not blue/purple unless used only as minor accents; the theme reads as educational/professional.
All user-facing text is in English.

file_operations.description: Define global theme tokens (CSS variables used by Tailwind/shadcn styling) and base typography/spacing defaults, selecting a non-blue/purple primary palette (e.g., greens/teals/neutral) suitable for educational/professional content.
Adjust Tailwind theme extensions (if needed) to support the chosen palette and consistent typography/spacing across pages while keeping blue/purple as at most minor accents.
Create a shared layout wrapper (header/title/navigation + consistent container spacing) used by both Gallery and Submit views to enforce consistent styling and English UI copy.
Wrap Gallery and Submit pages with the shared SiteLayout to apply the cohesive theme consistently.
